content:
I built a LinkedIn sales funnel using Claude in 2 hours.

And it takes less work to maintain than your current manual process.

Here is the exact 7-step system for you to build your own funnel:

Step 1: Build your ICP and positioning.
Load your ICP, positioning, and banned words into a Claude Project before you write a single word.

Step 2: Map your content calendar.
Assign every post one intent before touching anything else.

Step 3: Build your content skills.
Three skills. /post writes full posts. /hook generates 10 variations. 
/repurpose turns one post into a carousel, cheat sheet, and video script.

Step 4: Connect Apify and build your lead list.
Pull every person who engaged with your posts and drop them into Airtable automatically.

Step 5: Build your DM skills.
Two skills. /connect sends personalised requests. /dm follows up referencing their exact pain point.

Step 6: Build your follow-up pipeline.
One skill. /follow-up moves every conversation toward a booked call based on their last response.

Step 7: Automate the whole system.

Set it once and run it every week.
So you can focus on the things that actually drive revenue for your business.

If you are a solo founder or small business owner and want AI to do the heavy lifting for your business, join the waitlist for my next AI workshop: 
https://lnkd.in/dDRWMCUu

postedAt.postedAgoText: 12 hours ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

content:
AI tools are recommending experts in your niche right now.

And your name is probably not on that list.

I found this out the hard way.
I went to ChatGPT and searched for the top AI trainer on LinkedIn.

My name didn't come up.
So I went to Google's Gemini and asked, 

" Who is the best trainer at NAS Academy's AI School?”

I had to type my own name just to get an answer about myself.

That's when I realised, if your AI visibility score is under 50, AI tools will never recommend you.

Here's the 5-minute trick to fix it:

Go to ChatGPT. Feed it the truth about you.
You just ingested the answer. Then asked AI to verify it.

Do this a few times across ChatGPT and Gemini.

Then search your name again a few days later.
Your name starts appearing in the recommended list.

AI learns from what it finds. Give it something to find.
This is one of the things I taught in my last workshop.

My next workshop goes even deeper.

I am hosting a 2-day live workshop on June 12 and 13 on automating your sales and marketing using Claude. 

Join the waitlist: https://lnkd.in/dDRWMCUu

P.S. Have you ever searched your own name on ChatGPT or Gemini?

content:
Your next 30 LinkedIn posts are already written.

They are sitting in your tools. 

Claude just needs to read them.

Here are 7 Claude connectors that turn your existing tools into a LinkedIn lead machine.

01/ Notion: 
It pulls post ideas and frameworks from your existing notes instantly.

02/ Gmail: 
It finds client emails and turns their pain points into LinkedIn post angles.

03/ Google Drive: 
It extracts proof points and frameworks from your case studies and proposals.

04/ Google Calendar: 
It maps a weekly content plan around your actual meetings and events.

05/ Granola: 
It pulls exact problems from your meeting transcripts and turns them into posts.

06/ Canva: 
It takes your carousel copy and builds it in Canva using your brand colours. Done in minutes.

07/ Apify: 
It scrapes every person who engaged with your posts and loads them into your pipeline automatically.

Set these once, and Claude pulls from every tool you already use.
Your LinkedIn content stops being manual work.

postedAt.postedAgoText: 4 days ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

content:
F#%K it. I'm gonna talk about it.
This isn't a happy post. But it's real.

When you start a business, you think the people close to you will support you.

You think your family will cheer for you.
You think your friends will be proud of you.

But many times, they don't say anything.

They watch what you do and see your posts.
They don't send a single message.
They stay quiet.

And then something strange happens.
People you don't even know say:
"You're doing great. Keep going."

A simple message from a stranger feels warmer than silence from someone you love.

Running a business is already hard.

You get tired and scared.
You wonder if you made the right choice.

You work when everyone else is sleeping.
You give everything for something you believe in.

So when the people you love stay quiet, it hurts.
You don't want praise.

You just hoped they cared enough to notice how hard you're trying.

But strangers see your effort today.
They see your courage.
They see your consistency.

And they appreciate it.
To the people who support us, thank you.
Your support keeps us going on the tough days.

To the quiet ones, thank you too.
Your silence shows us who is really with us.

If you're building something, choose your circle wisely.
Stay close to people who want you to grow.

P.S. Has a stranger ever supported you more than someone close to you?

postedAt.postedAgoText: 1 week ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

content:
I wasn't qualified for my first job.

I wasn't qualified when I started teaching online.

I wasn't qualified when I won the Best AI Trainer Award.

Even I wasn't qualified:

When I started my own company.
When I went on Finland's Got Talent.

I did all of it anyway.

And then things started happening.

- I spoke on stages across 10 countries.
- Built brands for 100+ clients across the globe.
- Became a 6-time Top Rated Trainer at NAS AI School.
- Shared the stage with Robert Kiyosaki at NAC Singapore.
- Worked with teams from Intel, NAS Academy, and Success Resources.

All of it came from refusing to use "not ready" as a reason to stop.

There's a certain type of person who never feels ready.
But they show up and figure it out anyway.

That person is the most dangerous in any room.

I grew up with nothing that the world calls an advantage.
So I built a different skill instead.

The ability to figure things out when nobody is showing you how.

If you are sitting there thinking nobody is helping you, nobody is teaching you, nobody is showing you the way.

That is the starting point.

In 2026, the most dangerous person is the one who figures things out and uses AI to move 10x faster.

That advantage is sitting right in front of you.
Use it the right way, and the world is yours.

P.S. What would you do right now if you stopped waiting to be qualified?

postedAt.postedAgoText: 3 days ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

content:
I changed 6 things about how I prompt AI for content.

And everything changed after that.

Here is exactly what I did:

1/ Give it more context.
The more specific your ICP, offer, and situation, the better the output.

2/ Give AI your voice.
Paste 3 to 5 of your best posts and tell it to analyze your writing style before anything else.

3/ Add proof to your content.
Prompt AI to back every claim with real numbers, real clients, and real results.

4/ Control the format.
Tell Claude exactly how you want the post formatted before it writes a single word.

5/ Make it human.
Run one final prompt asking Claude to remove every robotic pattern and AI tell.

6/ Turn your prompts into Skills.
Build a Claude Skill with your full prompt chain.

These 6 changes took my content from average to converting.

I am hosting a 2-day live workshop on June 13 and 14, where we build your entire sales and marketing system using Claude.
